key_points.text: Compute Networking segment revenue surged 67% year-over-year to $193.5 billion in fiscal 2026, driven by accelerated computing and AI platform shifts, with Data Center computing up 59% and Data Center networking up 142%.

citation.quote: Compute Networking revenue – The year over year increase was driven by the major platform shifts – accelerated computing and AI. Revenue from Data Center computing grew 59% driven by demand for our Blackwell computing platform. Revenue from Data Center networking grew 142% driven by the introduction and continued ramp of NVLink compute fabric for GB200 and GB300 systems and the growth of Ethernet and InfiniBand platforms.

key_points.text: First quarter fiscal 2027 revenue reached $81.6 billion, up 85% year-over-year, with Compute Networking revenue at $74.6 billion (up 88% YoY) and operating income at $56.3 billion (up 138% YoY).

Data Center revenue in Q1 fiscal 2027 was $75.2 billion, up 92% year-over-year and up 21% sequentially, driven by the ramp of Blackwell 300 products and demand for InfiniBand, Spectrum-X Ethernet, and NVLink solutions.

citation.quote: Data Center $ 75,246 $ 39,112 92 % ... driven by the ramp of our Blackwell 300 products and demand for our InfiniBand, Spectrum-X Ethernet, and NVLink solutions.

key_points.text: Gross margin improved from 60.5% in Q1 fiscal 2026 to 74.9% in Q1 fiscal 2027, primarily due to lower inventory provisions following the prior year's $4.5 billion H20 excess inventory charge.

citation.quote: Gross margin increased from a year ago on lower inventory provisions, primarily due to the prior year's $4.5 billion charge associated with H20 excess inventory and purchase obligations.

key_points.text: Graphics segment revenue grew 57% in fiscal 2026 to $22.5 billion, driven by Blackwell architecture sales, with operating income increasing 80% to $9.2 billion.
Operating income in fiscal 2026 grew 58% to $139.3 billion, with the operating margin at 60.4%, showing strong profitability as the company scales.
The company announced an increase in quarterly cash dividend from $0.01 to $0.25 per share on May 18, 2026, payable June 26, 2026, reflecting confidence in cash generation and shareholder returns.

citation.quote: On May 18, 2026, we increased our quarterly cash dividend from $ 0.01 per share to $ 0.25 per share to all shareholders of record on June 4, 2026.

key_points.text: Hyperscale revenue remained at approximately 50% of Data Center revenue in Q1 fiscal 2027, while the remaining 50% came from diversified customers including AI Clouds, industrial, enterprise, and sovereign customers, reducing customer concentration risk.

citation.quote: Hyperscaler revenue increased sequentially and remained at approximately 50% of Data Center revenue, while the remaining 50% came from a continued diversification of customers, including AI Clouds, industrial, enterprise, and sovereign customers.
